From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

University in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan

University of Peshawar

The **University of Peshawar** ([Pashto](/wiki/Pashto_language "Pashto language"): د پېښور پوهنتون; [Hindko](/wiki/Hindko_language "Hindko language"): پشور یونیورسٹی; [Urdu](/wiki/Urdu_language "Urdu language"): جامعۂ پشاور; abbreviated **UoP**; known more popularly as **Peshawar University**) is a [public](/wiki/Public_university "Public university") [research university](/wiki/Research_university "Research university") located in [Peshawar](/wiki/Peshawar "Peshawar"), [Khyber Pakhtunkhwa](/wiki/Khyber_Pakhtunkhwa "Khyber Pakhtunkhwa"), Pakistan.[[1]](#cite_note-uniRank-1)

The university is one of the [oldest universities](/wiki/Universities_in_Pakistan#Khyber_Pakhtunkhwa "Universities in Pakistan") in the province, and is [ranked](/wiki/Rankings_of_universities_in_Pakistan "Rankings of universities in Pakistan") as one of the highest rated universities in the country.[[2]](#cite_note-HEC-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=University_of_Peshawar&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The university was founded in 1950 and offers programs for [undergraduate](/wiki/Undergraduate "Undergraduate"), [post-graduate](/wiki/Post-graduate "Post-graduate"), and [doctoral studies](/wiki/Doctoral_studies "Doctoral studies").[[1]](#cite_note-uniRank-1) With approximately 14,000 enrolled students attending the university, it has six academic faculties with forty postgraduate department as well as two "centers of excellence". The university is known for its research in [social](/wiki/Social_sciences "Social sciences"), [medical](/wiki/Medical_Science "Medical Science"), and [natural sciences](/wiki/Natural_sciences "Natural sciences") having eight research centers located inside the campus. Spread over an area of 1,045 acres (4 km2) as a residential campus, the university is the first [public university](/wiki/Public_university "Public university") to be established in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.

In its inaugural year, it had an enrollment of 129, of whom one was a woman.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] The university was founded as an expansion of the [Islamia College](/wiki/Islamia_College_(Peshawar) "Islamia College (Peshawar)"). The founder of Pakistan, [Muhammad Ali Jinnah](/wiki/Muhammad_Ali_Jinnah "Muhammad Ali Jinnah"), who had visited the college in 1928 had great admiration for it, referring to it as ‘my college’ and adopted it as one of the three heirs to his fortune, along with [Aligarh University](/wiki/Aligarh_University "Aligarh University") and Sindh Madrassa (his own school) in 1939. For Jinnah the university was a gift to students of the province, in recognition of the part played by them in the making of Pakistan, and how it was the [Pashtuns](/wiki/Pashtuns "Pashtuns") who had fought the Indians and captured [Azad Kashmir](/wiki/Azad_Kashmir "Azad Kashmir").

## Recognized university

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=University_of_Peshawar&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Recognized university")]

University of Peshawar is a recognized university by the [Higher Education Commission of Pakistan](/wiki/Higher_Education_Commission_of_Pakistan "Higher Education Commission of Pakistan"). This university awards both undergraduate and postgraduate degrees.[[2]](#cite_note-HEC-2)[[1]](#cite_note-uniRank-1)

## Departments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=University_of_Peshawar&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Departments")]

University of Peshawar has many departments including:

* Arts & Humanities
* Business & Social Sciences[[1]](#cite_note-uniRank-1)
* Language & Cultural
* Medicine & Health[[1]](#cite_note-uniRank-1)
* Science & Technology.[[1]](#cite_note-uniRank-1)

### Pashto Academy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=University_of_Peshawar&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Pashto Academy")]

Main article: [Pashto Academy](/wiki/Pashto_Academy "Pashto Academy")

### Department of Pharmacy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=University_of_Peshawar&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Department of Pharmacy")]

The department was established in 1982, housed at the Centre of Excellence in Physical Chemistry. After a period of two years, it was shifted to the newly constructed building of the present location. The department has been recognized by the [Pharmacy Council of Pakistan](/wiki/Pharmacy_Council_of_Pakistan "Pharmacy Council of Pakistan"). Research groups conduct research in the field of [Pharmacology](/wiki/Pharmacology "Pharmacology"), [Pharmaceutics](/wiki/Pharmaceutics "Pharmaceutics"), Pharmaceutical analysis and [Pharmacognosy](/wiki/Pharmacognosy "Pharmacognosy").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

An animal house with bioassay laboratory meets the research and academic needs of the university. Research in the Pharmacology section of the department is focused on testing compounds from both synthetic and natural origin for their utility in pain, inflammation, pyrexia, depression, anxiety, dependence, tolerance and vomiting as well as toxicological testing.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

### National Center of Excellence in Geology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=University_of_Peshawar&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: National Center of Excellence in Geology")]

The National Center of Excellence in Geology (NCEG), University of Peshawar (UoP), is an institution of higher learning and research in Geo-sciences. It was established in 1974 under an act of the parliament. Dr. [R.A. Khan Tahirkheli](/wiki/Rashid_Ahmed_Khan_Tahirkheli "Rashid Ahmed Khan Tahirkheli") was the founder Director of the center. Upon his retirement, Dr. [M. Qasim Jan](/wiki/M._Qasim_Jan "M. Qasim Jan") succeeded him in 1988 who was succeeded by Prof. Dr. Syed Hamidullah in January 2001, Dr. M. Asif Khan in 2002 and again by Dr. Syed Hamidullah in 2003. After the death of S. Hamidullah in an air crash, Dr. M. Asif Khan is the director of the center.

### Shaykh Zayed Islamic Centre

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=University_of_Peshawar&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Shaykh Zayed Islamic Centre")]

The Shaykh Zayed Islamic Center, University of Peshawar is one of the three educational Centers financed by his Excellency Shayh Zayed Ibne Sultan Al-Nahyan, Emir of the United Arab Emirates (UAE). The other two Centers are located at Karachi and Lahore on the Campuses of the respective universities. The entire cost of the project was borne by the Government of U.A.E while the land was donated by the University of Peshawar. The Emir of U.A.E has established a trust for the developmental expenditures of the center, while recurring cost is borne by the Federal Government of Pakistan.
